rulings.Case Title: ACCESS CAPITAL INVESTMENTS FUND TWO, LP VS. MATTHIAS RICHARD MEDERER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 4, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT KRISTEN BARTOK'S FINANCIAL DISTRESS DECLARATION is DENIED. The unlawful detainer complaint is not based on nonpayment of rent. Therefore, CCP section 1179.03(h) is inapplicable. The Declaration was also filed untimely, as it must be filed with 5 days of when the summons and legal papers are served on Defendant. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: SUTRO PROPERTY MANAGEMENT INC. VS. ANGUS GAVIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ***Re-Noticed*** Amended Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default, Default Judgment For Possession, And Judgment For Money Damages; Alternatively, For Relief From Court'S 12/14/2021 Order Denying Defendants' Motion To Set Aside The 10/6/2021 Money Judgment Obtained By Plaintiffs, As Well As The 1/5/2021 Notice Of Entry Of Default And 2 Clerk'S Judgment Dated 1/7/2021.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 4, 2022 line 9. DEFENDANTS ANGUS GAVIN AND JOHN GAVIN'S AMENDED MOTION TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT, DEFAULT JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION, AND JUDGMENT FOR MONEY DAMAGES; ALTERNATIVELY, FOR RELIEF FROM COURT'S 12/14/2021 ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE 10/6/2021 MONEY JUDGMENT OBTAINED BY PLAINTIFFS, AS WELL AS THE 1/5/2021 NOTICE OF ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND 2 CLERK'S JUDGMENT DATED 1/7/2021 is GRANTED. The examination of the judgment roll shows no evidence that jurisdiction was properly acquired. Plaintiffs were authorized by Court order to serve the Defendants by posting and mailing by certified mail. The Proof of Service filed on January 5, 2021 attests only to service "by posting and first-class mailing." There is no proof of service on file for certified mail, which is different from first-class mail. The default and default judgment are set aside. Since both sides admit that possession is no longer at issue Plaintiff may file and serve an amended complaint and obtain 30 day summons or file a dismissal no later than March 11, 2022. Defendants shall have statutory time to respond if Plaintiff files and serves an amended complaint and 30 day summons. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
